spec:
Watchdog timer with 32-bit countdown, kick (reload), and timeout detection.

Inputs:
- clk: system clock
- rst_n: active-low asynchronous reset
- enable: when high, countdown is active; when low, counter holds at load_val
- kick: pulse high for 1 cycle to reload the counter with load_val (pet the watchdog)
- load_val[31:0]: the value loaded into the counter on kick, timeout, or when disabled

Outputs:
- count[31:0]: current value of the countdown timer
- timeout: high when counter reaches 0 and enable is high

Behavior:
- After reset: count = 0
- When enable is low: count is continuously loaded with load_val (counter does not run)
- When enable goes high: counter decrements by 1 each clock cycle
- When kick is pulsed high: counter reloads to load_val immediately
- When counter reaches 0 (timeout): timeout goes high, counter auto-reloads to load_val
- Priority: reset > disable > kick > timeout > decrement

Verify: reset state, disabled behavior, countdown, kick reload, timeout detection, auto-reload after timeout, kick during countdown.
